reasoning: All three leading norovirus vaccine candidates are far from approval. Moderna's Phase 3 candidate experienced lower-than-expected case accruals and is extending its trial through the 2025-2026 Northern Hemisphere season, pushing efficacy data into 2026 at the earliest. HilleVax's advanced candidate failed its Phase 2b trial in infants in mid-2024. Vaxart's oral pill candidate is only passing Phase 1. Thus, there is zero credible prospect of a norovirus vaccine being approved and added to the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' by February 2026. The only minor risk components are Wikipedia vandalism / premature editing, or a completely unexpected regulatory approval for a niche adult candidate internationally. Given the short time horizons (Nov 16, 2025; Dec 9, 2025; Feb 7, 2026), the probability remains extremely low.
